mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes (Rank IC < 0.02) across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.2 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in over 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp, or the signal fails to predict direction with accuracy above 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and wealth effects, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 60-day returns in more than 60% of triggered cases, or the interaction term shows no incremental predictive power over individual series.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 20-day returns during early-cycle expansions, with a walk-forward Sharpe ratio below 0.5 for the signal.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls confirm economic momentum and corporate hiring strength, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero predictive correlation with 5-day forward returns, or the signal accuracy drops below 48% in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation accelerates beyond 2.5 percent annually, anticipated monetary tightening and margin compression force negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in over 65% of periods where CPIAUCSL YoY exceeds 2.5%, or the threshold signal yields a negative information coefficient.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly GDP expansion and rising retail sales validate broad-based economic demand, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined GDPC1 and RSAFS signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark over 20-day horizons, or interaction effects vanish in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in the industrial production index signal contracting manufacturing activity and reduced corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO changes consistently precede positive 5-day SPX returns, or the signal directional accuracy falls below 45% during late-cycle phases.
